questions.question: What is the atomic number of carbon?
questions.answer: The atomic number of carbon is 6.

questions.question: How many valence electrons does a carbon atom have?
questions.answer: Carbon has 4 valence electrons.

questions.question: What type of chemical bond is formed by carbon?
questions.answer: Carbon forms covalent bonds by sharing its valence electrons.

questions.question: What is catenation?
questions.answer: Catenation is the unique ability of carbon atoms to form bonds with other atoms of carbon, giving rise to large molecules.

questions.question: Define tetravalency of carbon.
questions.answer: Since carbon has a valency of four, it is capable of bonding with four other atoms of carbon or atoms of some other monovalent element.

questions.question: What are hydrocarbons?
questions.answer: All carbon compounds which contain only carbon and hydrogen are called hydrocarbons.

questions.question: What is the general formula for alkanes?
questions.answer: The general formula for alkanes is $C_nH_{2n+2}$.

questions.question: What is the general formula for alkenes?
questions.answer: The general formula for alkenes is $C_nH_{2n}$.

questions.question: What is the general formula for alkynes?
questions.answer: The general formula for alkynes is $C_nH_{2n-2}$.

questions.question: Name the first member of the alkane series.
questions.answer: Methane ($CH_4$).

questions.question: What are saturated carbon compounds?
questions.answer: Compounds of carbon which are linked by only single bonds between the carbon atoms are called saturated compounds.

questions.question: What are unsaturated carbon compounds?
questions.answer: Compounds of carbon having double or triple bonds between their carbon atoms are called unsaturated compounds.

questions.question: What are structural isomers?
questions.answer: Compounds with identical molecular formula but different structures are called structural isomers.

questions.question: What is a homologous series?
questions.answer: A series of compounds in which the same functional group substitutes for hydrogen in a carbon chain is called a homologous series.

questions.question: By what molecular mass unit do adjacent members of a homologous series differ?
questions.answer: They differ by 14 atomic mass units ($u$).

questions.question: Name the functional group present in alcohols.
questions.answer: The hydroxyl group ($-OH$).

questions.question: Name the functional group present in aldehydes.
questions.answer: The aldehyde group ($-CHO$).

questions.question: Name the functional group present in carboxylic acids.
questions.answer: The carboxyl group ($-COOH$).

questions.question: What is the common name of ethanoic acid?
questions.answer: Acetic acid.

questions.question: What is vinegar?
questions.answer: Vinegar is a 5-8% solution of acetic acid in water.

questions.question: What is esterification?
questions.answer: The reaction of an alcohol with a carboxylic acid to produce an ester and water is called esterification.

questions.question: What is saponification?
questions.answer: The process of preparing soap by the hydrolysis of fats or oils with an alkali is called saponification.

questions.question: What are micelles?
questions.answer: Micelles are clusters of molecules in which the hydrophobic tails are in the interior and the ionic ends are on the surface of the cluster.

questions.question: Why does soap not work in hard water?
questions.answer: Soap reacts with calcium and magnesium salts in hard water to form an insoluble substance called scum.

questions.question: Name two allotropes of carbon.
questions.answer: Diamond and Graphite.
